title: How do I set up passwordless authentication using Authenticator?
plain_text: With Microsoft Authenticator, you can sign in to your Microsoft account without using a password. To begin, you must set up and enable Microsoft Authenticator. Table of Contents Set up the Microsoft Authenticator app Enable passwordless sign-in with Microsoft Authenticator Enable phone sign-in Test Microsoft Authenticator Set up the Microsoft Authenticator app 1. Sign in to the to the My Account portal using your Northeastern account. 2. Select 'Security info' in the left menu. If you have already registered, you'll be prompted for two-factor verification. Then, select 'Add method'. 3. On the 'Add a method' page, select 'Authenticator app' from the list, and then select Add. 4. Install the official Microsoft Authenticator app on your mobile device and select Next. Note: For more information about how to download and install the app, see Download and install the Microsoft Authenticator app. 5. Remain on the Set up your account page while you set up the Microsoft Authenticator app on your mobile device. 6. Open the Microsoft Authenticator app, select 'allow notifications' (if prompted), select 'Add account' or + from the Customize and control icon on the upper-right, and then select Work or School account. Note: When setting up the Microsoft Authenticator app for the first time, it is recommended to allow camera access to scan the QR code. 7. Return to the Set up your account page on your computer, and then select Next. Then scan the QR code page. 8. Scan the provided code with the Microsoft Authenticator app QR code reader, which appeared on your mobile device after you created your work or school account in Step 6. Note: The authenticator app should successfully add your work or school account without requiring any additional information from you. However, if the QR code reader can't read the code, you can select 'Can't scan the QR code' and manually enter the code and URL into the Microsoft Authenticator app. For more information about manually adding a code, see Manually add an account to the app. 9. Select Next on the Scan the QR code page on your computer. A notification is sent to the Microsoft Authenticator app on your mobile device to test your account. 10. Approve the notification in the Microsoft Authenticator app, and then select Next. Your security info will be updated to use the Microsoft Authenticator app by default to verify your identity when using two-step verification or password reset. Enable passwordless sign-in with Microsoft Authenticator After you’ve registered for the Microsoft Authenticator app, you will need to enable phone sign-in. Enable phone sign-in 1. On your mobile device, open Microsoft Authenticator and select your Northeastern account. 2. Select Enable phone sign-in. 3. Follow the instructions in the app to finish registering the account for passwordless phone sign-in. You will need to enter your Northeastern account credentials. Test Microsoft Authenticator 1. Navigate to a Northeastern application that requires sign-in, like Workday. 2. Enter your username at the sign-in page. 3. Select Next. 4. If necessary, select Other ways to sign in. 5. Select Approve a request on my Authenticator app. You will be presented with a number on the screen, and your Microsoft Authenticator app will prompt you to authenticate by typing the appropriate number. After you have utilized passwordless phone sign-in, the app continues to use this method. However, you can still choose another method if needed.
body_html:
With Microsoft Authenticator, you can sign in to your Microsoft account without using a password. To begin, you must set up and enable Microsoft Authenticator.

Table of Contents

  • Set up the Microsoft Authenticator app
  • Enable passwordless sign-in with Microsoft Authenticator
    • Enable phone sign-in
    • Test Microsoft Authenticator

...

sections.heading: Set up the Microsoft Authenticator app
sections.text: Follow these steps to register the Microsoft Authenticator app with your Northeastern account.
steps: Sign in to the to the My Account portal using your Northeastern account.
Select 'Security info' in the left menu. Then, select 'Add method'.
On the 'Add a method' page, select 'Authenticator app' from the list, and then select Add.
Install the official Microsoft Authenticator app on your mobile device and select Next.
Remain on the Set up your account page while you set up the app on your mobile device.
Open the Microsoft Authenticator app and add a Work or School account.
Return to the Set up your account page on your computer, and then select Next.
Scan the provided code with the Microsoft Authenticator app QR code reader.
Select Next on the Scan the QR code page on your computer to test the account.
Approve the notification in the Microsoft Authenticator app, and then select Next.

links.text: Download and install the Microsoft Authenticator app
Manually add an account to the app

sections.heading: Enable phone sign-in
sections.text: After you’ve registered for the app, you will need to enable phone sign-in.
steps: On your mobile device, open Microsoft Authenticator and select your Northeastern account.
Follow the instructions in the app to finish registering the account for passwordless phone sign-in.

sections.heading: Test Microsoft Authenticator
sections.text: Verify the passwordless sign-in is working correctly.
steps: Navigate to a Northeastern application that requires sign-in, like Workday.
Enter your username at the sign-in page.
If necessary, select Other ways to sign in.
Select Approve a request on my Authenticator app and type the number shown on screen into the app.

Manually add an account to the app
